import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from scipy.stats import norm

def task_func(arr: np.ndarray) -> (plt.Axes, np.ndarray):
    # Calculate the sum of elements in each row of the array
    row_sums = arr.sum(axis=1)

    # Normalize the row sums to have a mean of 0 and a standard deviation of 1
    mean = np.mean(row_sums)
    std_dev = np.std(row_sums, ddof=1)
    normalized_data = (row_sums - mean) / std_dev if std_dev != 0 else np.zeros_like(arr)

    # Plot the histogram of the normalized data
    num_bins = 30
    fig, ax = plt.subplots()
    ax.hist(normalized_data, bins=num_bins, density=True, alpha=0.6, color='green')

    # Overlay the PDF of a standard normal distribution on the histogram
    pdf = norm.pdf
    x_range = np.linspace(-4, 4, num=200)
    y_range = pdf(x_range) * 1.96  # Cover 99% of a standard normal distribution
    ax.plot(x_range, y_range, linewidth=2, color='red')

    # Set the title of the plot
    ax.set_title("Histogram of Normalized Data with Standard Normal PDF")

    return ax, normalized_data
